stories.headline: US House votes to rein in Trump's war powers in rebuke over Iran
stories.summary: In the 215-208 vote, four Republicans joined Democrats to pass the measure, which had failed in three previous attempts.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon agree to implement ceasefire if Hezbollah stops attacks

stories.summary: The ceasefire comes as Israel has recently escalated its attacks and is staging its deepest ground offensive into Lebanon in two decades.

stories.headline: US House approves war powers resolution to rein in Trump's military actions
stories.summary: House lawmakers voted 215-208 on the resolution, with four Republicans joining Democrats, an outcome that saw cheers erupting in the House chamber in what is a clear rebuke of Trump’s war strategy.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes on residential buildings kill nine in Gaza City
stories.summary: Israeli strikes on residential buildings have killed nine Palestinians, setting homes ablaze and leaving destruction.

stories.summary: Options traders are ramping up hedges against sharp yen swings over the next two weeks, positioning for the Bank of Japan’s policy meeting and the possibility of currency intervention by authorities seeking to support the yen.

stories.headline: Nomura's Wang Thinks Asia Chips Rally Mostly Justified
stories.summary: Nomura International Wealth Management North Asia CIO Julia Wang says the chips rally in Asia is mostly justified by earnings growth, but she thinks valuation has also reset. Wang speaks with Haslinda Amin from the sidelines of 'Nomura Investment Forum Asia' in Singapore. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Korea’s Wealth Fund to Tap Alternative Assets With Tokyo Office
stories.summary: Korea Investment Corp. will open its first office in Tokyo this summer as the $232 billion sovereign wealth fund seeks to expand investments in Japan, including alternative assets such as private equity, hedge funds and private debt.

stories.headline: Sunshine Silver Raises $270 Million in US IPO to Restart Mine
stories.summary: Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining Co. raised $270 million in a US initial public offering that priced at the low end of the marketed range, with the funds expected to be used for restarting production at an Idaho mine.

stories.headline: Indian IT Stocks Slump as AI Jitters Resurface
stories.summary: Investors remain far from convinced that the sector has found a bottom.

stories.headline: Malaysian Tycoon Vincent Tan Cuts Berjaya Stake in Asset Sales
stories.summary: Malaysian tycoon Vincent Tan sold 115 million ringgit ($29 million) worth of shares in conglomerate Berjaya Corp. Bhd., extending a series of disposals that have reduced his holding in the company to about 15%.

stories.headline: Power Equipment Firm Innio’s Backers Raise $2.43 Billion in IPO
stories.summary: Gas engine manufacturer Innio Holding GmbH’s biggest shareholders raised $2.43 billion in an upsized initial public offering that priced at the top of the range.

stories.headline: New Zealand Home Building Hits 10-Year Low as House Prices Stall
stories.summary: New Zealand home-building has slumped to a 10-year low as a stagnating housing market and global uncertainty hit demand for new dwellings and alterations.

stories.headline: South Korea Vows Action in Case of Excessive Move as Won Weakens

stories.headline: Blackstone-Backed Liftoff Raises $437 Million in Revived US IPO

stories.headline: How Does LA Count Election Ballots? It Begins With a Dog Sniff.
stories.summary: Counting ballots in California is a complex and lengthy undertaking. In Los Angeles, the choreographed process includes canines, humans and machines.
